summary: An excellent audience recording captures a smoking second set with standout China > Rider, Terrapin > Jackhammer and a soaring Stella Blue.
review: Fans rave that the show is one of the best Omni performances of the early‑90s, highlighting an explosive second set – China > Rider, Terrapin > Jackhammer, The Other One and a beautiful Stella Blue.  The audience tape (Schoeps/Gardner) is praised as exceptionally clear and well balanced, reproducing the on‑stage energy with remarkable fidelity.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of reviews are 5‑star, lauding both musical performance and recording quality; only one mixed 3‑star review tempers the average slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Well‑balanced audience tape with clear instrument separation, strong stage ambience and minimal noise; some reviewers note occasional off‑notes/vocal slips but they do not detract from the overall fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A solid, melodic first set followed by a powerhouse second set; the band rides a tight groove, builds tension into The Other One and delivers an unforgettable Jackhammer climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia lets loose on Loser and adds soaring lead lines throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass bomb opening The Other One is described as a tsunami of low‑end power.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard work (Vince/Welnick era) is called “a force of nature,” with playful moments like handing off the opening China notes.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey and Bill are praised for their high energy, especially during the Jackhammer section and on‑stage camaraderie.
